_meta.description: Hand-drawn offshore shipping routes for common gasoline trade lanes. Each entry is a port-pair with a sparse, strategic waypoint list. Fewer waypoints means less visible angle-kinks when rendered as great-circle arcs on the client. Aim: 6-12 waypoints per route — key inflection points only (port, strait exits, strait/canal entries, ocean-crossing anchors, approach points).
_meta.key_format: sortedPortA, XX|sortedPortB, YY  — alphabetical to match distances.json keys
_meta.waypoint_format: [lat, lon] decimal degrees, ordered from first port (alphabetically lower) to second port
_meta.distance_policy: Distance is haversine-summed over waypoints at build time — no manual entry, stays honest
_meta.author: NEFGO ops team

_common_notes.ARA_to_NorthSea_exit: ARA ports (Amsterdam/Rotterdam/Antwerp) → Dover Strait at (51.00, 1.50), then Western Channel exit (49.5, -5.5)
_common_notes.Atlantic_N_crossing: English Channel exit → Celtic Sea (49, -10) → Mid-Atlantic peak ~50°N at -20/-30/-40W → Grand Banks south (43, -55) → US approach point (~40, -67)
_common_notes.Chesapeake_approach: Offshore (40, -70) → approach (37.0, -75.8) → bay entrance → up to Baltimore
_common_notes.Delaware_approach: Offshore (40, -70) → approach (39.0, -74.5) → bay entrance → up to Philadelphia

Amsterdam, NL|Baltimore, US.notes: ARA → Chesapeake Bay. Sparse waypoints = one big geodesic arc over Atlantic (peaks ~52°N real great circle).

Antwerp, BE|Baltimore, US.notes: ARA → Chesapeake Bay. Antwerp exit via Scheldt → Dover → single geodesic arc over Atlantic → Chesapeake approach.

Baltimore, US|Rotterdam, NL.notes: ARA → Chesapeake Bay (Rotterdam origin). Alphabetic sort puts Baltimore first.

Amsterdam, NL|New York, US.notes: ARA → New York via Newfoundland-south (42,-55) and Long-Island-south approach.

Amsterdam, NL|Philadelphia, US.notes: ARA → Delaware Bay (Philadelphia up the Delaware River).

Antwerp, BE|Philadelphia, US.notes: ARA → Delaware Bay. Antwerp exit via Scheldt.

Philadelphia, US|Rotterdam, NL.notes: ARA → Delaware Bay (Rotterdam origin). Alphabetical sort puts Philadelphia first.

Lavera, FR|New York, US.notes: Med → US via Gibraltar. Extra waypoint east of Cap de la Nau (Spanish peninsula) keeps geodesic offshore there, and (42, -55) keeps Atlantic crossing south of Newfoundland.

Barcelona, ES|Thessaloniki, GR.notes: Med-internal: Barcelona → south of Sardinia → Sicilian Channel (south of Sicily, between Tunisia & Malta) → Ionian → around Cape Matapan (Peloponnese) → up the Aegean to Thessaloniki. All waypoints verified offshore (earlier version cut across southern Sardinia at Cagliari).

Lagos, NG|Ust-Luga, RU.notes: Baltic → West Africa. Mirror of Antwerp-Lome for the Atlantic leg (safely offshore of the Sierra Leone/Guinea bulge) plus Baltic transit. Waypoints ordered Lagos → Ust-Luga (alphabetic).

Antwerp, BE|Lome, TG.notes: ARA → West Africa. Channel → offshore Biscay/Portugal → W of Canaries → W of Cape Verde latitudes → safely offshore Guinea coast → across open Gulf of Guinea → Lomé. Waypoints kept well offshore of the bulge between Sierra Leone and Ivory Coast — earlier version cut through Guinea/Sierra Leone interior and grazed Ivory Coast coast.
